source_title: Merchants of Grain (Dan Morgan) (z library.sk, 1lib.sk, z lib.sk)
l0_summary: To overcome multinational buyers' immense informational advantages, prairie farmers must leverage on-farm storage, monitor global macroeconomic shifts, and enforce strict pricing discipline to secure fair value.
l1_summary:
• Monitor macro indicators like ocean freight and currency shifts for early demand signals, as multinational buyers execute export deals before official reports.
• Proactively manage your flat price risk using transparent public futures; grain companies profit from logistics and basis, not local production costs.
• Maintain strict pricing discipline by knowing your exact cost of production and executing sales when margins are met rather than chasing rallies.
• Never tie farm credit or inputs to grain sales, which surrenders your marketing freedom and allows buyers to extract hidden premiums.
• Elevators blend grain to minimum specs and source globally; use on-farm storage strategically, as superior quality alone won't guarantee a premium.
